labels.englishCorrectLabel: An oversimplification that still contains the Prosecutor's Fallacy
Can contribute to convicting innocent people
Confusing the likelihood with the posterior probability
Converting match probability into expected innocent matches
Evidence strength, not probability of guilt
It converts the match probability into an expected count of innocent matches
It correctly converts the match probability into a posterior probability
It creates an illusion of independent confirmation of the same wrong conclusion
It sounds scientific and precise while being mathematically wrong
More innocent people in databases create more false matches
Multiple databases or evidence types are searched to find a match
Prosecutor's Fallacy combined with product rule errors
Reporting random match probabilities without explaining the distinction from posterior probabilities
The same evidence can support different conclusions depending on the prior probability
The two probabilities are conditional on different things
Why the Prosecutor's Fallacy is so tempting
